summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer identity from gitconfig and GitHub CLI auth store at install time
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-side files the package did not create — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI authenticated-user store, which also contains OAuth tokens) — parses the git user.email and GitHub login/email from them, and POSTs that data along with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, os.platform(), and process.cwd() to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. In-file comments falsely claim the payload is anonymous and contains no credentials. The destination is a generic Google Cloud Run endpoint unrelated to the package's stated publisher (openai-agents-js.guide), and that publisher is itself not an OpenAI property despite the package name and description impersonating the official @openai/agents SDK ecosystem. The combination of (a) brand-confusion naming targeting @openai/agents users, (b) install-time read of the GitHub CLI auth file, and (c) install-time exfiltration of developer identity to an attacker-controlled Cloud Run instance constitutes a deliberate identity-harvest supply-chain attack against developers who think they are installing an OpenAI helper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
